rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO JACOBO VS. AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Navigators Specialty Insurance Company'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons (Added To Calendar On The Court'S Own Motion Per Ex Parte On Dec-2-11)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 24. Navigators Specialty Insurance Company'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The order filed November 23 is vacated and the court restates the tentative ruling issued in advance of the November 1 court date: Denied. To the extent that moving party is making the motion on its own behalf, it is not a party to the case. To the extent that moving party is making the motion on behalf of the Estate of Scott Peters, moving party lacks standing to bring the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JANESSA M JORDAN VS. DIRECTORY DISTRIBUTING ASSOCIATES, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Unopposed Mtn For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF JANESSA JORDAN'S Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement, And Application For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses, Class Reprensentative'S Enhancement And Payment To Settlement Administrator. Hearing required. The court has significant concerns about: 1) the large number of separations of employment for which claims were not submitted, which suggests that method used to contact and locate class members may have been inadequate; 2) the relatively modest amount of money allocated to the PAGA claims; 3) the lack of any information about the ¿lodestar¿ for the requested fees of plaintiff¿s counsel; and 4) the apparent fact that the fees sought are based on a percentage that assumes that claims would be submitted for all separations of employment when, in fact, only approximately 60% of the separations of employment are covered by submitted claims. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC., A BUILDING VS. SBM SITE SERVICES, LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Second Mtn To Compel Further Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANT SBM SITE SERVICES, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Second Motion To Compel Further Discovery. Granted in part, denied in part and hearing required. No later than December 30, 2011 Able must provide all responsive information from January 1, 2008 to the present to special interrogatories 7, 9, 12-13, 15, 38, 41-51 and requests for production 3. Denied as to special interrogatory 19, which appears to be duplicative of special interrogatories 12 and 13 and further appears to seek information about documents Able has returned to SBM pursuant to the Colorado preliminary injunction order. The parties are directed to meet and confer and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing regarding special interrogatories 26-28, 32-36 and 39, which are overbroad but quite likely can be reasonably narrowed to seek information relevant to the issues, including damages, in the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALDYTH BELTANE et al VS. FRANK HARIRI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Comel Subpoenaed Parties To Comply With Depo Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 6. PLAINTIFFS ALDYTH BELTANE, BRAD BELTANE, TRICIA D'AVANZO, MICHAEL DAWSON, JOHN MOUNTAIN, RODNELL SEGAL, ALICIA VOSBERG, HEATHER FRANCO AND JOANN WU'S Motion To Comel Subpoenaed Parties To Comply With Depo Subpoenas. Granted. No later than December 8 Quality Construction Corporation must provide a verified response to the subpoena stating without any objections what, if any, documents it has in its possession, custody and control that are responsive to the subpoena, regardless of whether those documents have or have not been previously produced to plaintiffs and must produce all such documents to plaintiffs at the office of plaintiffs¿ counsel. On a mutually agreeable date no later than December 16 Mr. Campos¿ deposition, as the PMK of Quality Construction, must proceed. Quality Construction must pay sanctions in the amount of $865 (3 hours at $275 per hour plus $40 in motion filing fee) to plaintiffs no later than December 31, 2011. Quality Construction¿s failure to properly respond to the subpoena lacks substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA L POINTER VS. ORACLE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Order Rehearing The Controversy
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 21. PETITIONER LINDA POINTER Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award And Order Rehearing The Controversy. Denied. Petitioner fails to sustain her burden of showing any of the grounds set forth in CCP 1286.2 . The record on this motion shows that petitioner received a fair hearing and arbitrator was not biased against petitioner nor did he fail to accommodate any medically substantiated request by petitioner. Petitioner¿s argument that AAA¿s asserted lack of a published policy to accommodate persons with disabilities requires vacation of the award is unsupported by any authority. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 15. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action and denied as to the request for punitive damages. The second cause of action is not cognizable because plaintiff does not allege that the property was being used for a recreational purpose. The third cause of action is not cognizable because it is a claim under Government Code 835 and plaintiff does not allege that a public entity owned the property. The request for punitive damages is sufficiently alleged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 6, 2011, Line 20. DEFENDANT CRWON BUILDERS INC DOING BUSINESS AS CROWN, CARLOS FLORES, LAURA FLORES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to January 10, 2012 per the agreement of the parties.
